title: Move a DynArray into a Holder
prompt_md:
## Goal

A named object is an l-value, so passing it where a move is wanted requires
`std::move` to cast it to an r-value reference. Write a `Holder` class that
takes ownership of a move-only `DynArray` by moving it into a member.

The `DynArray` class is provided: it is move-constructible (the move nulls the
source) but **not copyable**. Your `Holder` must store a `DynArray` member and
initialize it by moving from the constructor argument.

## Input

- Line 1: an integer `n` (1 <= n <= 100), the size of the array.

## Output

- Line 1: `source: <size>` -- the source `DynArray`'s size after it has been
  moved into the holder (it should now be `0`).
- Line 2: `holder: <size>` -- the size the `Holder` now reports.

## Notes

Because `DynArray` is move-only, you must use `std::move` both when constructing
the `Holder` and inside the `Holder` constructor.

title: Swap via std::move
prompt_md:
## Goal

Two move-only objects can be exchanged without any copying using three moves.
Write `void swapBuffers(Buffer& a, Buffer& b)` that swaps the two buffers using
`std::move`: move one into a temporary, move-assign the second into the first,
then move-assign the temporary into the second.

The `Buffer` class is provided: it has a move constructor and move assignment
operator (both null the source) and deleted copy operations.

## Input

- Line 1: two space-separated integers `sizeA` and `sizeB`
  (1 <= each <= 100), the sizes of the two buffers.

## Output

- Line 1: `before: <sizeA> <sizeB>`
- Line 2: `after: <sizeB> <sizeA>` (the sizes after the swap)

## Notes

You may not copy a `Buffer` (copies are deleted) -- the swap must move.
